name: Community Vision Capital & Consulting
description_short: Community Vision Capital & Consulting is a California CDFI providing flexible real estate financing, advisory services, and investment opportunities for mission-driven nonprofits securing community assets.
description_long:
Community Vision Capital & Consulting is a certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) based in California that specializes in financing social purpose real estate projects. Founded on the principle that "place builds power," the organization partners exclusively with mission-driven nonprofits and community-rooted organizations to help them secure, preserve, and develop real estate that serves their communities.

The company offers three primary services: (1) flexible real estate lending tailored to nonprofits acquiring affordable housing, health clinics, cultural centers, and similar community assets; (2) culturally centered advisory services covering real estate strategy, financial management, and project execution from visioning through completion; and (3) structured investment opportunities for individuals and institutions seeking values-aligned returns in community development projects. As a licensed lender under California Financing Law (License #6050556), Community Vision arranges loans through both its primary entity and Credit Enhancement 1, LLC (License #60DBO-84210).

Community Vision distinguishes itself through its deep focus on community ownership and equity rather than profit maximization. The organization has deployed over $635 million in direct financing while leveraging an additional $3.3 billion in related investments and distributing $39 million in grants—demonstrating substantial capital deployment and community commitment. Their lending explicitly targets mission-driven organizations that conventional lenders often reject, offering more flexible terms and longer repayment horizons suited to nonprofit operations rather than traditional commercial real estate cycles.

The primary limitation is that Community Vision exclusively serves nonprofit organizations and community-rooted entities in California—not individual consumers or for-profit businesses. Their focus on social purpose real estate (affordable housing, health clinics, cultural centers) means they do not offer consumer mortgages or traditional residential lending. Additionally, as a CDFI serving underserved markets, their loan volume and geographic availability are significantly more limited than conventional mortgage lenders.
address: 870 Market St Suite 677, San Francisco, CA 94102

company_info.headquarters: 870 Market St Suite 677, San Francisco, CA 94102

pros: Licensed CDFI with $635M+ deployed in financing, demonstrating substantial capital availability and track record
Flexible lending terms specifically designed for nonprofit operations rather than conventional commercial standards
Integrated advisory services covering real estate strategy, financial management, and project planning throughout loan lifecycle
Leveraged $3.3 billion in related investments beyond direct lending, indicating strong ecosystem and partnership network
Distributed $39M in grants in addition to financing, providing non-debt capital support
Culturally centered approach to advising with explicit focus on equity and community power
Structured investment opportunities allowing individuals and institutions to support community development with market-rate returns
cons: Serves only nonprofit organizations and mission-driven entities—not available to individual consumers or for-profit businesses
Geographic focus limited to California only; no national lending presence
Specializes exclusively in real estate financing; cannot help with working capital, equipment, or other business lending needs
best_for: Nonprofit organizations seeking to purchase or preserve affordable housing, health clinics, or community cultural centers
Mission-driven community development corporations needing flexible real estate financing with longer terms than conventional lenders offer
Impact investors and institutions seeking values-aligned investment returns in California community development projects
diagnosis: Community Vision Capital & Consulting is exclusively for California-based nonprofit organizations and mission-driven entities seeking real estate financing for community assets like affordable housing and cultural centers—not for individual consumers, for-profit businesses, or those outside California. The main caveat is strict eligibility requirements: you must be a tax-exempt nonprofit organization with a community development mission to qualify.
services: Real estate financing for nonprofit acquisition of community assets
Preservation financing for existing mission-driven properties
Development financing for new social purpose real estate projects
Real estate strategy and visioning advisory services
Financial management advising for nonprofit organizations
Values-aligned investment opportunities for individuals and institutions
Grant distribution to advance community ownership
Culturally centered lending tailored to community-rooted organizations
Real estate due diligence and underwriting for nonprofits
Loan arrangement services through multiple licensed entities
